title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox celebrate doubles with a new routine inspired by a TikTok video from player Roman Anthony's high school days.
Manager Alex Cora explains the celebration is based on a TikTok dance Anthony performed to Mariah Carey's "Obsessed".
Roman Anthony is batting .217 with three extra-base hits in the Red Sox's first 12 games of the 2026 season.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kevin Scott Kelley, former Adams Police Chief, indicted for procurement fraud and false claims involving $19,123.15 in overtime pay.
Kelley allegedly filed fraudulent overtime claims under a municipal traffic enforcement grant linked to MassDOT from January 2024 to September 2025.
Adams Police noticed discrepancies, prompting an investigation by State Police; Kelley was placed on paid leave in September 2025.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions voted no confidence in Chief Charles Gray, urging his removal due to leadership failures.
Over 90% of union members criticized Gray for administrative issues and lack of body-worn cameras, impacting officer morale.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons, recently acquitted, plans a civil lawsuit; her case highlighted the department's lack of body cameras.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics reportedly interested in trading for Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo amid Milwaukee's struggles.
Boston's salary cap concerns could challenge any potential deal involving Antetokounmpo, under contract through 2028.
Antetokounmpo, a two-time MVP, may become a free agent after next season, increasing league interest in him.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox earned their first series win of the season with a 5-0 victory over the Milwaukee Brewers.
Sonny Gray pitched 6 1/3 scoreless innings, improving his ERA to 2.76 and strengthening Boston's starting rotation.
Rookie Tyler Samaniego struck out the side in his major league debut, marking a strong start for the reliever.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget for Boston's fiscal year 2027, increasing spending by 2.1%.
Wu's budget proposal addresses inflation, rising costs, and slowing revenues, reducing discretionary funding and freezing some spending.
Boston's proposal includes $1.7 billion for schools, aligning staffing with enrollment decline to maintain a 10-to-1 student-teacher ratio.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the 2026 Patriots Hall of Fame induction.
Gronkowski is a first-time finalist; Mankins and Vinatieri have been finalists multiple times previously.
Fans will select the inductee from the three finalists, with voting concluding on April 28th.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract, set to debut Saturday at TD Garden.
Hagens scored one goal and three assists in six games with Providence Bruins, showcasing strong skating and playmaking skills.
Joining Bruins' roster, Hagens could provide depth and offensive spark for Boston's playoff campaign.

title: Sen. Markey, local clergy call for end to Iran war and Trump’s time in office
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and Boston clergy urged an end to U.S. involvement in the Iran war and removal of Trump.
The call for change was made following a U.S.-Iran two-week ceasefire after 39 days of conflict.
Local activists and peace groups condemned Trump's rhetoric and military strategies during Boston Common event.

title: Readers say their electric bills are ‘outrageously high.’ Here’s how much they pay.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A Boston.com poll shows 200 out of 239 respondents find their electric bills unmanageable.
Readers report electric bills have significantly increased, with some doubling over the past year.
Many residents consider municipal utilities for lower costs, but availability varies across Massachusetts.
